markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose history, revisions, actor changes, conflicts, and EventLedger receipts to operator/model surfaces. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentHistoryView leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentHistoryView is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: History endpoint unbounded; returned full content_json of every version (response-size DoS)
evidence.fix: GET /history paginated (default 50, cap 200) returning metadata only + total_versions; new GET /history/:doc_version lazy single-body load; storage gains version-meta list (content_json not selected), count, and single-version getters
evidence.tests: knowledge_documents_api_tests 7 passed incl. mt156_history_is_paginated_and_omits_version_bodies (page [2,3] of 5, no content_json keys, limit clamp 100000->200, lazy body load, 404 missing version)

findings.title: History endpoint is unbounded and returns full content_json of every version
findings.evidence: list_knowledge_rich_document_versions (knowledge.rs:4601) has no LIMIT/pagination; load_history (api:532-559) returns every version row, each carrying full content_json. A large, long-lived document returns its entire revision history (all bodies) in one response - unbounded query + response-size DoS on a hot route.
findings.fix: Paginate versions (LIMIT/OFFSET or keyset) and omit content_json from the list (return version metadata + sha; lazy-load a single version body on demand).

missing_tests: test: history of a doc with many large versions paginates and does not return all bodies inline

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-156 (DocumentHistoryView) requires exposing document history, revisions, actor identity, and EventLedger receipts to operator/model surfaces with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers exactly this: real PG-backed history endpoints (paginated metadata list + lazy single-version body load), permission-gated, exposing version sha/receipt event ids and owner actor identity, with a real end-to-end PostgreSQL integration test and a frontend history+diff view wired to the real authority.
findings: load_history (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_documents.rs:1122) and load_history_version (:1167) are gated by ctx.require(DocumentAction::Read) and read real rows from the knowledge_rich_document_versions PostgreSQL table; the list response exposes versions metadata, total_versions, current_version, authority_label, owner_actor_kind/id, and per-version content_sha256 + promotion_receipt_event_id (EventLedger receipt) — satisfying MT scope 'history, revisions, actor changes, ... EventLedger receipts'.

Adversarial-v2 DoS hardening is real (commit bde6d28f): GET /history is paginated (HISTORY_DEFAULT_LIMIT=50, HISTORY_MAX_LIMIT=200, knowledge_documents.rs:381-383) and returns metadata only — list_knowledge_rich_document_version_metas (storage/knowledge.rs:4681) deliberately does NOT select content_json; count_knowledge_rich_document_versions and get_knowledge_rich_document_version provide total + lazy single-body load with 404 on missing version.

Runtime proof is real and managed-PostgreSQL backed: mt156_history_is_paginated_and_omits_version_bodies (tests/knowledge_documents_api_tests.rs) builds a 5-version doc via real HTTP saves on a doc_server backed by PostgresDatabase::connect, asserts page [2,3] with content_json absent, limit 100000 clamps to 200, GET /history/3 returns the v3 body, and GET /history/99 returns 404. knowledge_pg_support.rs resolves to POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL or the Handshake-managed no-Docker cluster with NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (returns None -> SKIP if PG truly absent).

No deferred-live escape: grep of the impl + storage path found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/placeholder/stub reachable from the history behavior; source comments explicitly assert 'NO mocks, no SQLite'.

Frontend wired to real authority: app/src/lib/api.ts:3274 loadRichDocumentHistory and :3287 loadRichDocumentVersion target the real endpoints; RichDocVersion type omits content_json (matches metadata-only list) and RichDocVersionBody adds it (lazy load). RichDocumentView.tsx renders a revision history + version-diff surface (data-testid rich-document-history) with vitest coverage in RichDocumentView.test.tsx.

No forbidden paths: no SQLite/Elasticsearch/OpenSearch/Meilisearch/Typesense/rusqlite usage in the touched files (only negation comments). All changes confined to src/backend/handshake_core and app/src per packet allowed_paths.
Aside (non-blocking, governance paperwork): MT contract lifecycle.status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and validator_verdict is unset; that is expected wind-down paperwork and not a code reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
